turns.user_message: They just sighed and walked out of the room when I tried to explain how I felt. I sat there staring at the closed door, wondering why my words always seem to bounce right off them. It has been happening for months now, and I keep telling myself it is just a rough patch. I guess I am just tired of feeling like my voice does not matter in this space. I went to make some tea to calm down, but my hands were shaking the whole time.

Every time I bring up how that moment made me feel, they just sigh and change the subject like it never happened. I have started keeping a mental tally of all the times I swallowed my own thoughts just to keep the peace. It is exhausting to constantly walk on eggshells around someone who refuses to acknowledge the tension. I catch myself rehearsing conversations in my head, trying to find the perfect wording that will not trigger another shutdown. The silence in the house is getting heavier, and I do not know how to fill it anymore.

I heard that same heavy sigh echo through the hallway today, and it completely shattered whatever patience I had left. I have spent so long bending myself into shapes that fit their comfort zone that I barely recognize my own reflection anymore. Every conversation feels like a minefield where I am the only one getting burned by the fallout. I sit in the quiet and replay every single time I backed down, wondering when I stopped fighting for my own peace. It is suffocating to live in a space where my presence is tolerated but never truly seen.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This has been going on for a while.
